Questionnaire: Ensure to complete the questionnaire with full and accurate details. Any gaps will not be   accepted.
Passport with all stamped pages: This includes pages with your photo, name, signature, date of birth, place of birth, place of issue, address, etc. Copies of your old and current passport on which travelled to other countries showing entry/exit immigration stamps.
All Permits ever held in Canada: Permits issued as visitor/student/worker to be attached.
Digital photo as per specifications Perm: Provide front and back of the picture- refer to the instructions mentioned in the link below. More information here: https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/new-immigrants/pr-card/apply- renew-replace/photo.html
Proof of language proficiency (IELTS- G/:
Language test results must not be older than two years upon date of receipt. For English tests must have CLB 7 or above in all
language abilities. Minimum of 4 for each English/French language skill.
Medical Exam:
Only Panel Physicians approved by Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada can do this exam.
Find a Panel Physician: https://secure.cic.gc.ca/PanelPhysicianMedecinDesigne/en/Home
More information here: https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/application/medical-police/medical-exams/requirements-permanent-residents.html
Police clearance certificates (PCC):
A police certificate is a statement that you don’t have a criminal record or, if you have one, a copy of your criminal record.
They help us confirm if there are any reasons you may be inadmissible to Canada. Please provide PCC from all countries
where you have lived more than 6 months after turning 18-year-old or in last 10 years. The PCC issue date must be within
the last 6 months or after the most recent arrival in Canada, whichever is earlier.
For India: Only Passport Seva Kendra PCC is accepted.
How to get a police certificate: https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/application/medical-police/police-certificates/how.html

Proof of work experience for the claimin:
• All paystubs/payslips for the claiming experience- Paystubs summarize an employee's gross pay, taxes,
deductions, and net pay. It may be issued as a paper document alongside a pay cheque or provided
electronically OR bank statements showing salary deposits OR letter(s) from employer(s) confirming your annual
salary/hourly wage (Salary Certificate).
• T4 or also known as Statement of Remuneration Paid,  is    a tax slip that employers issue to employees after each
calendar year OR Form 16.
• Notice of Assessment- an evaluation of your tax return that the Canada Revenue Agency sends you every year
after you file your tax return OR any other tax payment proof in that country (E.g. Income Tax Returns)
• Employment/Reference letter for all periods of qualifying work experience you identify in your application.
Requirements for the Employment letter: (We can share a template upon request)
o Must be printed on company letterhead. Company’s contact information: address, telephone number,
website address and email address,
o Name of the person signing the letter,
o Title of the signing authority,
o Signed by the responsible officer/supervisor (Signatures should be hand-signed or, if electronic, must
closely match the original or be done through DocuSign. Typed names are not acceptable.)
o All positions with their job title held at the company,
o Detailed description of duties and responsibilities for each position,
o Tenure of each position,
o Mention if it was a full-time or part-time position,
o Number of work hours per week,
o Current Annual salary or hourly rate, also mention your salary at the time of hiring.
Identity and Civil Documents:
Legal documents showing name or date of birth changes (if applicable)
Common Law declaration- imm5409 (if applicable)
Marriage Certificate, Final Divorce or Annulment Certificate. If married more than once, include certificates from each
marriage and divorce you have had (if applicable)
Death certificate for former spouse or common law partner (if applicable)
Birth certificate of children (if applicable)
Marriage affidavit (with couple picture) if marriage certificate is not available
One and same name affidavit if name /sur:
In case you have different names in your official documents you will need an Affidavit of One and the Same Person (We
can share a template upon request). This is a legal document used to confirm that an individual is known by different
names. Affidavits can be sworn (stamped and signed) by practicing lawyers or notaries public. Also provide proofs for name
changed legally as instructed by the government in your country.


It should be issued by the government and should have parents name on it.

signatures of both parents are required.

Alberta job offer must all be for an eligible occupation.

statements showing salary deposits.

calendar year

Job Offer Letter::
Please ensure it includes the following elements:
The letter must be less than six months old and printed on business letterhead, displaying the business address,

It
should identify the responsible officer or supervisor, including their signature.

date.

it i s for a full-time position of indeterminate duration (no end date) and state that the terms are
effective as of the date of nomination for current employees, or upon obtaining a work permit for new hires.
Must be signed by you and your employer.

Letters, Printed text messages, emails,:
contact. (We can share the templates upon request)
1. A detailed letter that tells the story of the couple's relationship, starting from when they first met up to now,
highlighting important moments and changes along the way.
2. At least 30-50 Random pictures of the couple from various events, wedding, celebrations, private pictures that
prove the genuineness of the relationship.
3. Wedding invitation cards from both sides of the document.
4. Screenshots of emails, texts, WhatsApp chats, frequent phone calls, proof of video calls, chats, and Facebook
Messenger calls that show the genuine nature of their relationship.
5. Social media screenshots from Facebook, Instagram, and others showing their long-term friendship and interaction.
6. Evidence of financial interdependence through wire transfer transactions.
7. Documents like medical or life insurance with both names, and information on joint accounts, insurances, or other
shared financial arrangements.
8. Records like photos, receipts, and screenshots of gifts exchanged, along with handwritten or typed romantic letters.
9. Boarding passes, Hotel bookings or tickets confirming travel to meet each other.
10. Proof of living together- Lease agreement, joint utility bills, joint bank account, credit card statement with same
address and government issued ID cards for both.
11. Letters of support for sponsorship
- At least 2 letters of support from family or friends who know your relationship
and can affirm that you are a genuine couple. Letters of support confirming the authenticity of the couple's
relationship can be provided by close family members such as parents or siblings, longtime friends familiar with
their bond, roommates or colleagues who have observed them together socially and professionally, and neighbours
who interact with them regularly. Additionally, community or religious leaders, family friends, and mentors or
teachers who have witnessed their relationship over time can offer valuable testimony.
PR Card or eCOPR: This includes front and back of your PR Card or the COPR copy that is signed by you and the Immigration officer.
T4:
T4/Form 16 or also known as Statement of Remuneration Paid,  is    a tax slip that employers issue to employees after each
calendar year.
Employment/ Source of Income: Original employment letter from employer stating period of employment, salary, regular hours.
Paystubs:
Paystubs summarize an employee's gross pay, taxes, deductions, and net pay. It may be issued as a paper document
alongside a pay cheque or provided electronically.
Canadian Education Documents- (For each:
Provide complete academic records and credentials. This typically includes:
Official Marksheet: A detailed report card or official transcript showing the grades or marks obtained in each subject for
every year or semester of your academic program.
Degree Certificates: Official documents issued by educational institutions verifying the completion of a course, degree,
diploma, or other educational qualifications.
Proof/source of Income- Mandatory for Wo:
o Job letter from current employer which includes start date, job title, salary, hours worked, job duties (mandatory),
signed by the employer on the company letterhead (Signatures should be hand-signed or, if electronic, must closely
match the original or be done through DocuSign. Typed names are not acceptable.)
o At least three pay slips also known as wage statements or pay statements. Paystubs summarize an employee's
gross pay, taxes, deductions, and net pay. It may be issued as a paper document alongside a pay cheque or
provided electronically,
o T4 issued by the employer,
o Bank statement for last 3 months with good funds.
Additional proof of Funds/investments/as:
If you have funds/investments/assets that will help you increase your net worth, please disclose them and provide
documentation to support.
Proof of cohabitation:
Drivers license (front and back),
Lease Agreement,
Most recent credit card statement,
Most recent Utility Bill- Electricity, Gas.
Proof of Admission:
o Unconditional Letter of Acceptance (LOA): An unconditional offer letter from a college is a formal acceptance that guarantees
admission, as the applicant has met all the required criteria, with no further conditions to fulfill before enrolling.
o Official Fee receipt: An official fee receipt from a college is a document that confirms all tuition and associated fees for one
year have been fully paid, serving as proof of payment for the applicant's enrollment.
o Enrollment Letter:  An enrollment letter from a college is an official document confirming a student's acceptance and
registration into a specific program or course of study, outlining important details such as start dates and when you expect to
finish the academic program.
o Current Timetable:  Provide current timetable if you are currently enrolled.
o Official/unofficial Transcripts:  This will show how many courses were taken in each semester and if you were a full/part time
student.
o Official/unofficial break letter: Please provide proper supporting documentation/ email confirmation that proves you had a
break while studying.
Proof of financial support while you stu:
Bank statement – At least for last 3 months with good funds. This will prove that you have enough funds to support yourself if you
stay longer.
Proof of employment- If you are working provide most recent dated employment letter and at least 3 recent paystubs.
Support Affidavit- Provide financial and source of income documents from immediate family members who will support you
financially. A notarized affidavit is required to support the application. (We can share a template upon request)
Please note: TDOT Case Officer may ask for additional proofs.
Upfront Medical exams – Check with us be:
for an upfront medical exam.
Only Panel Physicians approved by Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada can do this exam.
Find a Panel Physician: https://secure.cic.gc.ca/PanelPhysicianMedecinDesigne/en/Home
Do an upfront medical exam from which you don’t need any letter from IRCC asking for it.
More information here: https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/application/medical-police/medical-exams/requirements-temporary-residents.html
All Marksheet and certificates:
Provide complete academic records and credentials. This typically includes:
Marksheet: A detailed report card or transcript showing the grades or marks obtained in each subject for every year or
semester of your academic program.
Certificates: Official documents issued by educational institutions verifying the completion of a course, degree, diploma, or
other educational qualifications.
Completion Letter: Official letter issued by the institution that states that you have completed the course.
 Documents for the Dependent Applicant:
Recommendation Letters:
A recommendation letter for higher studies abroad should briefly introduce the recommender, their relationship with the
applicant, and how long they've known them. It should highlight the applicant's academic strengths, personal qualities like
leadership and teamwork, and provide specific examples of their achievements or contributions.
Statement of Purpose:
A Statement of Purpose in a study permit application is a written document where the applicant explains their motivation
for choosing a specific program, how it aligns with their academic and career goals, and why they wish to pursue it in the
selected country or institution.
We encourage our clients to provide us with a draft of their SOP to better understand their perspective.

Proof/source of Income - Mandatory:
o Bank Statements- Provide at least 6 months bank statements without any suddenly deposited funds. Ensure to provide
all the bank statements where you have good funds.
o Investments- Provide supporting documentation that show the investment is in your name from a specific period.
o Assets- In case of properties- provide most recent Property Evaluation Report, it specifies in detail about the property
and its current market value.
o In case of Gold- provide most recent gold evaluation report issued by a Jeweller on his letterhead with signature and
stamp.
o Net worth Certificate/ CA Report- it is prepared on a letterhead along with stamp and signature of the Chartered
Accountant. He matches the bank statement, current market value of property and current market value of gold as per
the reports mentioned above in his Report. This report will help the officer understand the total net worth of the
applicants.
o Support Affidavit- if the assets mentioned above belong to immediate family members and they are going to support
you for this application. A notarized affidavit is required to support the application. (We can share a template upon
request)

international students deposit a specific amount, typically required for proof of funds, to cover living expenses during their
first year in Canada.

A police certificate is a statement that you don’t have a criminal record or, if you have one, a copy of your criminal record. They
help us confirm if there are any reasons you may be inadmissible to Canada. Please provide PCC from all countries where you
have lived more than 6 months after turning 18-year-old or in last 10 years. The PCC issue date must be within the last 6 months
or after the most recent arrival in Canada, whichever is earlier.
For India: Only BLS/Passport Seva Kendra PCC is accepted.

$100,000 of emergency coverage and be valid for a minimum of 1 year from the date of entry.

Educational Credential Assessment – Serv:
Educational Credential Assessment (ECA) reports are used to check and compare an applicant’s foreign education to Canadian
standards. These reports help decide if someone gets immigration points or is eligible for certain programs.
• Comparative Education Service (CES), University of Toronto School of Continuing Studies
• International Credential Assessment Service of Canada (ICAS)
• International Credential Evaluation Service(ICES)
• International Qualifications Assessment Service (IQAS)
• World Education Services (WES)
More information here: https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/corporate/partners-service-providers/immigrant-serving-organizations/best-practices/foreign-educational-credential-assessment.html

A recommendation letter should talk about a person's skills, strengths, and qualities, showing why they are a good fit for
the job or opportunity. It should give examples of their work or achievements. The letter should end with a strong
statement of support, showing confidence in their ability to do well.

documents may lead to refusal.

Language Test Report:
Language test results must not be older than two years upon date of receipt.
International graduates from a bachelor's, master's, or doctoral program will need to achieve CLB 7 in English or NCLC 7 in
French in all four skill areas (listening, speaking, reading, and writing). Graduates from college programs or other university
programs must meet CLB 5 or NCLC 5 in all four areas.
Language tests accepted: For English- CELPIP- G, IELTS-G, PTE Core. For French- TEF Canada and TCF Canada.
For more information: https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/study-canada/work/after-graduation/eligibility/study-requirements.html
